briefs.title: Proposed: Maple Ridge Council Territory Acknowledgement — No Business Impact
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to formally recognize that the city conducts its business on the traditional and unceded territories of the Katzie and Kwantlen First Nations. This is a ceremonial acknowledgement only and does not propose any new rules, bylaws, or requirements for local businesses.
briefs.industries: Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to Consider Memorial Tribute for Deputy CAO Stephane Labonne
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council will consider a memorial tribute honouring Stephane Labonne, who served as Deputy Chief Administrative Officer (the second-highest administrative official at City Hall). This is a ceremonial agenda item and does not propose any new rules, bylaws, or requirements affecting local businesses.
briefs.industries: Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to Consider: FortisBC Deep Energy Retrofit Presentation at Maple Ridge
briefs.summary: At the April 14, 2026 Maple Ridge Council meeting, FortisBC is set to present on its Deep Energy Retrofit program — a program that helps building owners make major energy efficiency upgrades. Council will hear the presentation as part of its regular agenda, which could signal future City support or incentives for local businesses and property owners pursuing energy retrofits.
briefs.action_required: Watch for follow-up Council decisions on energy retrofit incentives that could affect your building costs.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on new election rules, sign limits, and business park tax repeal
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to vote on several items at its April 14, 2026 meeting, including a proposed new Election Bylaw that would consolidate existing election rules, add mail ballot procedures, and set new election sign limits — and a separate bylaw that would repeal a landscape maintenance tax (a special annual charge on property owners) at Kanaka Business Park, ending the levy for owners of Lots 1–51.
briefs.action_required: If you own property at Kanaka Business Park (Lots 1–51), watch for the Council decision on the tax repeal.

key_dates.label: Maple Ridge 2026 General Local Election day
Metro 2050 North 256 Street amendment comment period closes

briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Maple Ridge Consent Agenda Items — April 14, 2026
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to vote on receiving the items listed in the April 14, 2026 Regular Council Consent Agenda into the official record. A consent agenda is a package of routine items grouped together for a single vote — no detailed discussion is expected unless a councillor requests otherwise. No specific business-impacting measures are described in this item.
briefs.industries: Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on reappointing Integrity Commissioner for two more years
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to vote on reappointing Ms. Anita Atwal for another two-year term as Integrity Commissioner — the independent officer who investigates complaints about councillor conduct. Council will also consider delaying a related education session until after the new Council is sworn in this November 2026.
briefs.industries: Professional Services

briefs.title: Proposed: Maple Ridge Industrial Land Expansion Near 256 Street Up for Regional Approval
briefs.summary: Metro Vancouver's regional board has started the formal process to consider rezoning about 301 hectares of land near North 256 Street in Maple Ridge from rural and industrial to industrial and conservation uses. If approved, this proposed amendment to Metro 2050 — the region's long-term growth plan — would add roughly 198 hectares of new industrial land and expand the Urban Containment Boundary (the line that separates urban development areas from rural land), potentially opening the area to a light industrial hub.
briefs.action_required: Submit written comments to Metro Vancouver by May 18, 2026 at jonathan.cote@metrovancouver.org.

key_dates.label: Metro 2050 North 256 Street Industrial Lands amendment — comment period closes

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Natural Resources

briefs.title: Maple Ridge Advisory Committees Review Crosswalk Safety and Transportation Updates
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge advisory committees met in early 2026 to review several city initiatives, including a major expansion of pedestrian crosswalk safety beacons (from 13 to over 82 locations), a speed reader board program to encourage safe driving, and updates on planning bylaws. These are internal committee meetings where staff and members share information and provide input — no binding decisions affecting businesses were made at these sessions.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to vote on ending Kanaka Business Park landscape maintenance fee and new election rules
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to vote on two proposed bylaws at its April 14, 2026 meeting. The first would cancel a special property tax (a per-lot charge paid by owners) that has funded shared landscaping at Kanaka Business Park since 2009, after a majority of property owners signed a petition to end it. The second would create a new, consolidated election bylaw covering mail-in voting and election sign rules for the October 2026 local election.
briefs.action_required: If you own property at Kanaka Business Park (Lots 1–51), watch for the council decision — your tax bill could change.

key_dates.label: Maple Ridge general local election day
Metro 2050 amendment comment period closes — North 256 Street Industrial Lands

briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Retail

briefs.title: Council to vote on repealing Kanaka Business Park landscape maintenance tax levy
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to vote on repealing a bylaw that charges property owners in the Kanaka Business Park a special annual tax — called a parcel tax levy — to pay for landscape maintenance. More than half of the 51 affected property owners signed a petition asking for the charge to be cancelled, and Council will now decide whether to scrap it entirely.
briefs.action_required: If you own property at Kanaka Business Park (Lots 1–51), watch for the Council decision — it affects your annual tax bill.

key_dates.label: Maple Ridge Council vote — Kanaka Business Park landscape levy repeal bylaw

briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Retail

briefs.title: Proposed: Maple Ridge to consolidate election bylaws and introduce new election sign rules
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to vote on two proposed bylaws that would combine existing election rules into one updated bylaw and introduce new regulations for election signs ahead of the 2026 General Local Election. The proposed changes would also allow mail-in voting with clear rules, and move all election sign rules out of the general Sign Bylaw into the new Election Bylaw — with no limit on the number of signs, larger signs allowed on private property, and smaller signs (18"x24") permitted on city boulevards.
briefs.action_required: If you plan to post election signs, watch for the council decision — new sign rules may affect where and what size you can post.

key_dates.label: Maple Ridge Election Bylaw No. 8096-2026 — first, second, and third reading vote
Maple Ridge Sign Amendment Bylaw No. 8101-2026 — first, second, and third reading vote

briefs.industries: Professional Services, Retail

briefs.title: Council to Consider Short-Term Rental Year-One Update and Continuation in Maple Ridge
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to receive a year-one update on the city's Short-Term Rental (STR) program, which launched in April 2025. Staff are recommending that Council keep the current rules in place for another year — including the requirement that rentals must be in the owner's primary home, a minimum lot size of 371 m², and a limit of six guests — before considering any changes such as allowing STRs in townhouses or apartments.
briefs.action_required: If you operate or plan to operate a short-term rental in Maple Ridge, register with the Provincial STR Registry now.

key_dates.label: Maple Ridge STR Provincial Registry registration — compliance required

briefs.industries: Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Maple Ridge Short-Term Rental Program Year One Update
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to receive a year-one update on the city's Short-Term Rental (STR) program, which launched in April 2025. Staff report the program is working well — 49 licences issued, 25 applications under review — and recommend keeping the current rules in place for another year before considering any expansion.
briefs.action_required: If you run or plan to start an STR in Maple Ridge, check the City's Operator Guide to confirm you meet current licence requirements.

key_dates.label: Maple Ridge STR Provincial Registry registration required — takes effect

briefs.industries: Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to Consider McKenney Creek Restoration and Enhancement Plan — Maple Ridge
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to receive an update on the McKenney Creek Restoration and Enhancement Plan — a study aimed at improving the health of the creek while managing the effects of new development and climate change. No final plan has been approved yet; staff will return to Council with a recommended plan for direction at a future meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the future Council meeting when the full McKenney Creek plan comes forward — it may affect development requirements near the creek.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Agriculture, Natural Resources

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Maple Ridge Efficiency Program Saves $342K, Speeds Up Permits
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council will consider receiving an information report on its Continuous Improvement Centre of Excellence — an internal city program launched in June 2024 to cut waste, speed up services, and save money. The report highlights $342,761 in estimated savings so far, including an 84% reduction in building permit processing times, which directly affects business owners and developers waiting on approvals.
briefs.action_required: Watch for faster building permit timelines — apply online to take advantage of the 84% processing time reduction.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Professional Services, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to vote on appointing election officers for Maple Ridge's 2026 General Local Election
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to vote on appointing Carolyn Mushata as Chief Election Officer and Tonya Polz as Deputy Chief Election Officer to run the 2026 General Local Election, scheduled for October 17, 2026. These roles are required by provincial law and will oversee all election planning, including securing voting locations and recruiting election workers.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision if your business is near a potential voting location.

key_dates.label: Maple Ridge 2026 General Local Election takes place

briefs.industries: Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Ending Kanaka Business Park landscape maintenance levy
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to vote on repealing the Kanaka Business Park Landscape Maintenance Bylaw — a special property tax (called a parcel tax levy) that has charged business park owners for shared landscaping since 2009. More than half of the 51 affected property owners signed a petition to end the levy. If approved, the tax would stop immediately for 2026, with existing reserve funds covering maintenance costs for roughly 15 more years before general city taxes take over.
briefs.action_required: If you own property at Kanaka Business Park (Lots 1–51), watch for the council vote — this affects your 2026 tax bill.

key_dates.label: Maple Ridge Kanaka Business Park landscape levy repeal — council vote

briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Retail

briefs.title: Proposed: Maple Ridge Council to vote on short-term rental update, election bylaws, and business park tax repeal
briefs.summary: At its April 14, 2026 meeting, Maple Ridge Council is set to vote on several items affecting local businesses: a proposed bylaw would end a landscape maintenance tax (a special annual charge on property owners) at Kanaka Business Park; a new consolidated Election Bylaw would set rules for mail-in voting and election signs for the October 2026 local election; and Council will receive a year-one update on the city's short-term rental licensing program, which has issued 49 licences so far.
briefs.action_required: If you own property at Kanaka Business Park or operate a short-term rental, watch for the council decision — it may affect your taxes or licence.

key_dates.label: Maple Ridge 2026 General Local Election — voting day
Metro 2050 North 256 Street Industrial Lands amendment — comment period closes

briefs.industries: Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Council to vote on new Maple Ridge election bylaw, sign rules, and STR update
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to vote on several items at its April 14, 2026 meeting, including a proposed new Election Bylaw that would consolidate existing election rules and add mail ballot procedures for the October 2026 local election, updated election sign rules, and a repeal of a landscape maintenance fee at Kanaka Business Park. Council will also receive a year-one update on the city's short-term rental (STR) licensing program, which has issued 49 licences since April 2025.
briefs.action_required: If you operate a short-term rental or plan to run in the 2026 election, watch for council decisions on STR rules and new election sign regulations.

Metro Vancouver comment period closes — North 256 Street Industrial Lands Area Plan amendment

briefs.industries: Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Council to vote on ending special landscape tax at Kanaka Business Park
briefs.summary: Maple Ridge Council is set to vote on repealing a bylaw (a local law) that has charged property owners at Kanaka Business Park a special annual tax — called a parcel tax — to fund shared landscape maintenance since 2009. More than half the property owners in the area signed a petition to end the tax. If approved, the tax would stop immediately for 2026, and general city taxes would eventually fund the maintenance instead.
briefs.action_required: If you own property at Kanaka Business Park, watch for the council vote — this affects your 2026 tax bill.

key_dates.label: Kanaka Business Park landscape parcel tax repeal — council vote
